observations.sourceLabel: User recalls two manual resets roughly one to two days apart; this action aligns with a 42,730-snapshot schedule claim.
observations.notesPublic: The reset action is probable, not independently confirmed. Whether its seven-day phase survives later resets is the experiment.

observations.sourceLabel: User recalls a second manual reset soon after the first; this action aligns with a 4,970-snapshot schedule claim.
observations.notesPublic: The reset action is probable, not independently confirmed. The July 25 deadline remains a parallel-phase test, not a promise.

observations.sourceLabel: The live backend exposed a seven-day main window ending 29 July 09:19:23 NZ, fixing its start at this instant.
observations.notesPublic: This establishes the window transition and next deadline. It does not by itself identify the trigger.

timingComparisons.referenceLabel: UTC representation of the observed July 22 reset
timingComparisons.notesPublic: These are the same instant. UTC says July 21 while Pacific/Auckland says July 22.
The observed window start was 43 minutes 50 seconds later. Timezone conversion cannot explain this difference.
The observed window start was 11 hours 21 minutes 44 seconds later.
The observed window start was 27 hours 45 minutes 23 seconds later, so timezone alone does not align this anchor.

creditInventorySnapshots.notesPublic: This is the only recovered structured inventory snapshot. It proves two credits were available then, but not why they were granted.
